config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena sejarah sering menunjukkan bahwa alasan "demi masyarakat" bisa dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menginjak martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan menghormati hak individu sambil mencari keseimbangan yang adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that is exactly how governments and majorities can excuse abuses in the name of the common good. From my perspective in the United States, individual rights are supposed to be the baseline that society has to justify limiting, not something society can override whenever it is convenient. That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order sometimes require limits, but those limits should be narrow and carefully checked. If we flip the rule and put society first by default, we risk turning personal liberty into a privilege instead of a right.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi ketertiban” atau “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman, diskriminasi, atau penyalahgunaan wewenang, jadi hak individu harus tetap jadi batas yang jelas. Memang benar ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang menuntut pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, proporsional, dan bisa diuji, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan hak dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling sering menekan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak orang sambil tetap mengurus kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. I do think society can sometimes limit individual rights for things like public health, safety, or preventing harm, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. If we start from the idea that society comes first by default, then individual rights stop being rights and become permissions granted by whoever has power. A healthy society protects people first and then asks how the common good can be achieved without trampling basic liberty.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia alasan “kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas dan melegitimasi tindakan sewenang-wenang. Bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan sesuatu yang mudah disingkirkan ketika mayoritas merasa punya alasan yang kuat. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, proporsional, dan bisa diawasi, bukan dijadikan kebiasaan. Kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan, yang sering terjadi justru bukan solidaritas, melainkan kekuasaan yang menutupi pelanggaran terhadap warga.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority by default, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. I do not deny that public health, safety, and preventing real harm can justify limits on individual rights, but those limits should be narrow, temporary, and accountable to law. Once society becomes the automatic trump card, individual rights stop functioning as rights and start looking like privileges that power can grant or take away. A free society should begin with protecting the person, then justify any exception very carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
